headline: GOLD HOLDS $4,700 AS IRAN STRIFE PERSISTS; Deutsche Bank Eyes $6,000 Target
snippet: Gold climbed back above $4,700 per ounce Monday after Iran submitted a new Strait of Hormuz proposal, reversing earlier session losses driven by stalled peace talks. Deutsche Bank maintains a $6,000/oz target as gold extends a year-to-date gain of over 25% against a weakening dollar.

headline: MAPLE GOLD Delivers Maiden 4.53 g/t Joutel Underground Resource; Abitibi Project Grows to 4.28Moz Inferred
snippet: Maple Gold Mines (TSXV: MGM) added 394K indicated and 1.77M inferred ounces to its Abitibi-region Douay/Joutel project, establishing a maiden Joutel underground resource grading 4.53 g/t Au for 992K oz inferred. Total project now stands at 905K oz indicated (1.55 g/t) and 4.28Moz inferred.

headline: DISCOVERY SILVER Takes 9.9% Strategic Stake in Abitibi Metals via C$30.75M Non-Brokered Financing
snippet: Abitibi Metals Corp. (CSE: AMQ) closed a C$30.75M non-brokered financing with Discovery Silver Corp. as lead investor, taking a 9.9% strategic stake in the copper-gold junior. The deal grants Discovery participation rights in future financings as Abitibi advances its B26 polymetallic deposit.

headline: AMERICAS GOLD & SILVER Outlines $60-80M Galena Complex Plan to Triple Milling Capacity to 1,200 stpd by Year-End
snippet: Americas Gold & Silver (TSX: USA / NYSE American: USAS) detailed a US$60–80M 2026 capital plan at its Idaho Galena Complex targeting a jump in milling capacity from 750 to 1,200 stpd by year-end. A paste backfill plant targets Q4 commissioning while a separate US$30–40M budget drives Crescent Mine rehabilitation.

headline: URANIUM ENERGY Gets Final Texas Approval to Begin Burke Hollow ISR Production; UEC Stock Surges 8.83%
snippet: Uranium Energy Corp. (NYSE: UEC) received final regulatory approval from the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality to begin production at Burke Hollow — described as the largest new greenfield ISR uranium discovery in the U.S. in a decade, with licensed processing capacity of four million pounds annually. Shares surged 8.83% on the news.

headline: THACKER PASS Build Gains Steam: Lithium Americas Shares Jump 7.65% as Nevada Construction Accelerates
snippet: Lithium Americas Corp. (TSX/NYSE: LAC) gained 7.65% as Thacker Pass construction in Nevada picks up pace, backed by a DOE loan and GM equity stake with employment ramping toward 1,800 workers. Phase 1 targets first lithium carbonate output by late 2027, positioned to benefit from the current supply squeeze where lithium carbonate prices have doubled from late-2025 lows.

headline: NDPR ALLOY UP 138% YEAR-TO-DATE as Rare Earth Supply Deficit Enters Second Consecutive Year
snippet: NdPr alloy has surged 138% year-to-date as the rare earth supply deficit runs into its second consecutive year. Terbium posted its best single-month gain since 2023, rising 20.7% to $970/kg in April, while global EV demand — forecast at 22.9M units in 2026 — keeps structural pressure on critical supply chains.

headline: PERSEUS MINING Fires First Production Blast at CMA Underground — Côte d'Ivoire's First Mechanised Underground Mine
snippet: Perseus Mining (ASX/TSX: PRU) completed the first underground production blast at its Yaouré CMA project in Côte d'Ivoire, a landmark milestone as the country's first mechanised underground mine. Higher-grade underground ore will supplement open-pit mill feed, with commercial production targeted for Q3 FY 2027.

headline: REPORT: U.S. GOVERNMENT Quietly Buying 'Cartel Gold' — What It Signals for Demand and Price Discovery
snippet: Money Metals Exchange examines reports that U.S. government entities have been quietly purchasing gold through cartel-adjacent supply channels in Latin America, analyzing what this unusual buying activity suggests about institutional gold demand and the limits of mainstream price discovery for retail investors.

headline: OIL-GAS CRISIS Accelerates the Nuclear Case: How the Iran War and AI Data Centers Are Reshaping Uranium Investment
snippet: CNBC examines how the Iran-driven energy crisis and surging AI data center electricity demand are converging to strengthen the long-term nuclear power thesis, with spot U3O8 holding above $86/lb and utility demand re-emerging after years of under-contracting. Analysts flag uranium equities as repricing materially.

headline: BUYERS SHOP THE DIP: Gold Reverses $4,700 Session Losses as Silver Surges 5.38% on Iran Breakthrough Reports
snippet: GoldSeek's market commentary tracks gold's recovery above $4,700 and a sharp 5.38% silver rally after Iran submitted new Strait of Hormuz proposals mid-session Monday. The reversal illustrated how thin the sell-side bid is at current levels, with dip-buyers stepping in aggressively on any Iran de-escalation headline.
